feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: beta — BAY tuh
entry.summary: beta is pronounced "BAY tuh" (/ˈbeɪ tə/). US: "BAY-tuh". UK: "BEE-tuh". Greek β. Release channel + Bayes notation.

entry.title: delta — DEL tuh
entry.summary: delta is pronounced "DEL tuh" (/ˈdɛl tə/). "DEL-tuh". Greek δ. Diff/change semantics + git-delta pager.

entry.title: epsilon — EP suh lon
entry.summary: epsilon is pronounced "EP suh lon" (/ˈɛp sə lɒn/). "EP-suh-lon". Greek ε. Floating-point epsilon, ε-greedy.

entry.title: mu — myoo
entry.summary: mu is pronounced "myoo" (/mjuː/). "myoo". Greek μ. Statistical mean.

entry.title: sigma — SIG muh
entry.summary: sigma is pronounced "SIG muh" (/ˈsɪɡ mə/). "SIG-muh". Greek σ. Standard deviation, sum operator.

entry.title: pi — pye
entry.summary: pi is pronounced "pye" (/paɪ/). "pye". Greek π. 3.14159… Also Raspberry Pi.

entry.title: theta — THAY tuh
entry.summary: theta is pronounced "THAY tuh" (/ˈθeɪ tə/). US: "THAY-tuh". UK: "THEE-tuh". Greek θ. Angles + ML params.

entry.title: kappa — KAP uh
entry.summary: kappa is pronounced "KAP uh" (/ˈkæp ə/). "KAP-uh". Greek κ. Twitch chat meme + Cohen's kappa.

entry.title: zoxide — ZOAK side
entry.summary: zoxide is pronounced "ZOAK side" (/ˈzoʊk saɪd/). "ZOAK-side" — like zinc oxide. Smarter `cd`.

entry.title: bat — bat
entry.summary: bat is pronounced "bat" (/bæt/). English "bat". cat clone with syntax highlighting.

entry.title: hyperfine — HYPER fyne
entry.summary: hyperfine is pronounced "HYPER fyne" (/ˈhaɪ pər faɪn/). "HYPER-fyne". CLI benchmarking tool.

entry.title: tokei — toh KAY
entry.summary: tokei is pronounced "toh KAY" (/toʊˈkeɪ/). "toh-KAY". Japanese 時計 (clock). Counts lines of code.

entry.title: tealdeer — TEAL deer
entry.summary: tealdeer is pronounced "TEAL deer" (/ˈtiːl dɪər/). "TEAL-deer". tldr client written in Rust.

entry.title: difftastic — diff TAS tik
entry.summary: difftastic is pronounced "diff TAS tik" (/dɪfˈtæs tɪk/). "diff-TAS-tik". Structural diff.

entry.title: jq — J Q
entry.summary: jq is pronounced "J Q" (/ˌdʒeɪ ˈkjuː/). Letters "J-Q". JSON processor.

entry.title: yq — Y Q
entry.summary: yq is pronounced "Y Q" (/ˌwaɪ ˈkjuː/). Letters "Y-Q". YAML/JSON/XML processor (jq spinoff).

entry.title: Cosmos — COZ mus
entry.summary: Cosmos is pronounced "COZ mus" (/ˈkɒz məs/). "KOZ-mus". L1 blockchain.

entry.title: Polkadot — POLE kuh dot
entry.summary: Polkadot is pronounced "POLE kuh dot" (/ˈpoʊl kə dɒt/). "POLE-kuh-dot" — the dance/pattern.

entry.title: Cardano — kar DAH no
entry.summary: Cardano is pronounced "kar DAH no" (/kɑːrˈdɑː noʊ/). "kar-DAH-no". After Gerolamo Cardano (mathematician).

entry.title: Avalanche — AV uh lanch
entry.summary: Avalanche is pronounced "AV uh lanch" (/ˈæv ə læntʃ/). "AV-uh-lanch". L1 blockchain.

entry.title: Aptos — AP toss
entry.summary: Aptos is pronounced "AP toss" (/ˈæp tɒs/). "AP-toss". Move-based L1.

entry.title: Sui — swee
entry.summary: Sui is pronounced "swee" (/swiː/). "swee" — like the wind. Move-based L1.

entry.title: Optimism — OP tih miz um
entry.summary: Optimism is pronounced "OP tih miz um" (/ˈɒp tə mɪz əm/). "OP-tih-miz-um". Ethereum L2 rollup.

entry.title: Arbitrum — AR bih trum
entry.summary: Arbitrum is pronounced "AR bih trum" (/ˈɑːr bɪ trəm/). "AR-bih-trum" (Offchain Labs) common; "ar-BIT-rum" also heard.

entry.title: Wasmtime — WOZ um time
entry.summary: Wasmtime is pronounced "WOZ um time" (/ˈwɒz əm taɪm/). "WOZ-um-time" — wasm + time. WebAssembly runtime.
